title: trichophyllum viviparum
side_effects: Trichophyllum viviparum can have side effects if used improperly, such as diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting, due to its high toxicity when ingested in large quantities. Prolonged exposure to its extracts has also been linked to liver damage and kidney failure. Improper use of the plant can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems, including coughing and shortness of breath. In severe cases, overdose or prolonged exposure can lead to coma and even death.
